title: Can You Trust the Machine?

modules.title: When AI Sounds Totally Confident
modules.sub: Spotting certainty that hides a shaky foundation
modules.description: Learners explore real examples of AI responses that sound authoritative but contain errors or gaps. They practice noticing the language patterns AI uses when it is overconfident, and begin building a mental radar for suspicious certainty.

modules.title: The Story That Was Almost True
modules.sub: One wrong fact can change everything in a story
modules.description: Set inside a bustling newsroom simulation, learners receive an AI-drafted article and must hunt for the detail that is plausible but false. Their editorial choices determine whether the flawed story goes to print or gets corrected before it causes harm.

modules.title: How AI Learns to Hallucinate
modules.sub: Why AI invents facts it has never actually seen
modules.description: Learners get a plain-language look under the hood at how large language models generate text, and why that process sometimes produces confident-sounding fiction. This module turns the concept of hallucination from a mystery into something understandable and predictable.

modules.title: Your Turn: Fact-Check the Bot
modules.sub: Roll up your sleeves and challenge the AI yourself
modules.description: Learners interrogate a series of AI-generated claims across different topics, deciding which need verification and then using provided sources to check them. Each correct catch earns a clue toward assembling their personal verification checklist.

modules.title: When to Trust, When to Verify
modules.sub: Making smart judgment calls about AI-generated information
modules.description: Learners work through a decision framework that helps them sort AI outputs into trust, verify, or reject categories based on stakes, source type, and how checkable a claim is. They apply the framework to a fresh set of newsroom scenarios before the final module.

modules.title: Build Your AI Truth Toolkit
modules.sub: Create your own checklist for evaluating AI claims
modules.description: Drawing on everything from the previous modules, learners assemble a personalised verification checklist they can actually use in school and everyday life. They finish by stress-testing their toolkit against one final AI-generated report, proving it works before they leave the course.

synopsis: Step inside a newsroom where an AI assistant keeps feeding your team information — some true, some dangerously wrong — and every choice you make affects the story that gets published. By the end, you'll have built a personal checklist for deciding when to believe what AI tells you.
rationale: As AI-generated answers replace search results in everyday student research, the inability to critically evaluate AI outputs is rapidly becoming a core literacy gap with direct academic and safety consequences.
learning_outcome: Students can apply a structured verification process to AI-generated claims, identifying hallucinations, unsupported confidence, and when to seek a second source.
source_signals: Modern-Day Oracles or Bullshit Machines? How to thrive in a ChatGPT world
Tali Horowitz: The Key Issue with AI Chatbots and Teens
The Risks of AI in Schools Outweigh the Benefits, Report Says
